2026-05-01.notes: The chicken appears to be a good portion, likely half a small chicken. The salad is a substantial mix of beetroot and celery, possibly with other vegetables or dressing contributing to fat/carb content.

2026-05-01.mealName: Skim Milk Cappuccino and Chocolate Croissant
2026-05-01.notes: Assumed a standard chocolate croissant (approx. 80-90g) and a typical large skim milk cappuccino (250ml milk). Milk calculations are for skim milk. Croissant nutritional data is based on averages for a chocolate pastry.
Assumed a standard Fishbowl 'OG Chicken' bowl with chicken, brown rice, standard mix of veggies (carrots, cabbage, edamame), and a typical dressing (e.g., miso sesame or shoyu).

2026-05-01.mealName: Sourdough Bread with Cream Cheese and Gouda Cheese
2026-05-01.notes: Assumed 1 medium slice (approx 50g) sourdough bread, 2 tablespoons full-fat cream cheese, and 1 slice (approx 20g) gouda cheese.
Assumed a standard 250ml (approximately 8.5 fl oz) cappuccino made with skim milk and no added sugar or syrup. Coffee itself contributes negligible calories.

2026-05-02.mealName: Charcoal Gluten Bread with Bacon and Poached Eggs
2026-05-02.notes: Assumed 2 slices of charcoal gluten bread (~160 calories total), 2 medium poached eggs (~140 calories total), 2 strips of bacon (pan-fried, ~100 calories total), and 1 tablespoon of butter (~100 calories).

Assumed a standard-sized (around 1.5-2 oz) chocolate chip cookie with sea salt. Nutritional content can vary significantly based on recipe ingredients (e.g., type of flour, amount of butter/sugar/chocolate, inclusion of eggs).

2026-05-02.mealName: Two slices of Diavola pizza with scampi
2026-05-02.notes: Assumed two medium slices of Diavola pizza (spicy salami, mozzarella, tomato sauce) and a side of scampi (shrimp in garlic butter sauce). Nutritional values are an approximation based on common restaurant portions and recipes. Scampi portion assumed to be around 4-6 large shrimp. Pizza could vary significantly in size and topping amount.

Assumed a single serving consistent with a main course at a restaurant like Bills. 'Crispy' implies deep-frying or pan-frying, adding fat. 'White pepper chicken' likely includes a sauce, rice or similar starch, and potentially a small side salad or garnish. Fat content is higher due to crispy preparation. Carbohydrates include starch and potential sugars in the sauce. Protein is from the chicken itself.

Assumed a typical main course portion of prawn pasta at a restaurant like Bills, including pasta, a creamy or oil-based sauce, prawns, and potentially some vegetables like cherry tomatoes or spinach. Pasta type assumed to be standard white pasta. 'Bills' restaurants are known for generous portions and sometimes richer dishes.

Assumed 15 individual nigiri-style salmon sushi pieces (rice ball with salmon on top), with each piece averaging 50 calories, 5g protein, 7g carbs, and 1g fat. No mayo means no additional fat from that source.
Based on 2 servings of Lindt EXCELLENCE 70% Cacao Sea Salt Dark Chocolate, with ~20g per serving.

2026-05-03.notes: Based on 200ml skim milk and a small amount of coffee. Assumes no added sugar. Nutritional values are approximations.
